message:
Add agent connector scaffolding for six supported tools

Establish connectors module structure with placeholder implementations
for all six supported coding agent integrations: Codex CLI, Claude Code,
Gemini CLI, Cline, Amp, and OpenCode.

Module purpose (per PLAN § 2, § 3.1, § 7):
Connectors are the data ingestion layer, responsible for detecting
whether each agent is installed on the system, locating its conversation
history storage (JSONL files, JSON checkpoints, SQLite databases, VS Code
extension state), parsing agent-specific formats, and normalizing
heterogeneous data into the unified Conversation/Message schema defined
in model::types for persistence in SQLite and indexing in Tantivy.

Per-connector responsibilities (planned, per PLAN § 7):

codex.rs - OpenAI Codex CLI connector (PLAN § 7.2):
- Detection: Check for codex binary in PATH (via which crate) or
  ~/.codex directory existence
- History location: $CODEX_HOME/sessions/YYYY/MM/DD/rollout-*.jsonl
  with fallback to $CODEX_HOME (default ~/.codex), optionally scan
  $CODEX_HOME/history.jsonl if history.persistence enabled in config
- Format: JSONL with one event per line (user_message, assistant_message,
  tool_run, approval events), reconstruct conversation from event stream
- Normalization: external_id = session UUID or file path, workspace =
  working_directory field from session metadata, started_at/ended_at =
  first/last event timestamp, role mapping (user_message → User,
  assistant_message → Agent, tool_run → Tool)
- Incremental updates: notify watcher on $CODEX_HOME/sessions for new
  rollout-*.jsonl files, parse and upsert via source_path uniqueness

claude_code.rs - Anthropic Claude Code connector (PLAN § 7.3):
- Detection: ~/.claude directory existence, optional VS Code extension
  check via globalStorage presence
- History location: ~/.claude/projects/**/*.jsonl session logs, plus
  per-repo .claude/.claude.json files for embedded transcripts
- Format: JSONL with session-id field grouping events into conversations,
  map user/assistant/tool roles, extract title from first user message
  or task name metadata
- Normalization: workspace = project path if embedded in events,
  external_id = session-id, handle JSONL line errors gracefully (skip
  malformed lines, log warning)
- Watch: ~/.claude/projects for new/modified .jsonl files

gemini.rs - Google Gemini CLI connector (PLAN § 7.4):
- Detection: gemini or gemini-cli binary in PATH, or ~/.gemini directory
- History location: ~/.gemini/tmp/<project-hash>/ directories containing
  checkpoint-*.json and chat-log-*.json files per project
- Format: JSON checkpoints store conversation state, chat logs store
  message history (prefer logs, fallback to checkpoints), each project-
  hash directory = one project cluster
- Normalization: external_id = project-hash + checkpoint id, title =
  first user message or model-provided session name, timestamps from
  message created_at fields
- Watch: ~/.gemini/tmp for new project directories and JSON file updates

cline.rs - Cline VS Code extension connector (PLAN § 7.6):
- Detection: VS Code globalStorage path existence at platform-specific
  location (~/Library/Application Support/Code/User/globalStorage/
  saoudrizwan.claude-dev on macOS, ~/.config/Code/User/globalStorage/
  saoudrizwan.claude-dev on Linux, %APPDATA%\Code\User\globalStorage\
  saoudrizwan.claude-dev on Windows)
- History location: Per-task directories within globalStorage containing
  task_metadata.json (title, provider, workspace root), ui_messages.json
  and api_conversation_history.json (transcript), optional taskHistory.json
  index (may be corrupted per Stack Overflow issues, rebuild from task
  directories if missing)
- Format: JSON files per task, reconstruct conversation from ui_messages
  or api_conversation_history arrays ordered by timestamp
- Normalization: external_id = task UUID (directory name), workspace =
  task_metadata.json workspace field, role mapping from provider-specific
  message types
- Watch: globalStorage directory for new task directories

amp.rs - Sourcegraph Amp connector (PLAN § 7.5):
- Detection: amp binary in PATH (npm global install), or ~/.local/share/amp
  (Linux/macOS) or %APPDATA%\amp (Windows) existence
- History location: Limited—Amp stores threads primarily on Sourcegraph
  servers, only partial cache in VS Code globalStorage (extension-managed)
  and CLI config directory. Scan both globalStorage and ~/.local/share/amp
  for any JSON/JSONL logs
- Format: Underdocumented, exploratory scanning for JSON with thread/
  message structure, may need to reverse-engineer from cache files
- Normalization: external_id = thread id if found, metadata_json flag
  partial=true to indicate incomplete conversation history, UI should
  display caveat per PLAN § 2.4
- Watch: globalStorage and CLI cache directories if detected

opencode.rs - OpenCode CLI connector (PLAN § 7.7):
- Detection: opencode binary in PATH (optional), scan CWD upward for
  .opencode directories (project-local), check ~/.config/opencode for
  global database if configured
- History location: .opencode/<config>/<database.sqlite> per project,
  or global SQLite database path from config
- Format: SQLite schema with sessions, messages, files tables (introspect
  schema at runtime via rusqlite PRAGMA table_info), one session row =
  one Conversation, messages JOIN to session
- Normalization: external_id = session.id, workspace = .opencode parent
  directory (project root), map session/message rows to Conversation/
  Message structs, snippets from files table if present
- Incremental: track SQLite mtime and last-imported row id/timestamp,
  query for newer rows on change (no notify watcher, periodic poll)

Shared abstractions (PLAN § 7.1):
All connectors will implement common traits (to be defined):
- DetectionResult: enum (Detected{paths}, NotFound, ConfigOverride)
- fn detect_installation(env: &Environment) -> DetectionResult
- fn scan_sessions(ctx: &ScanContext) -> anyhow::Result<Vec<Conversation>>
- fn watch_paths(ctx: &ScanContext, tx: Sender<IndexUpdate>) -> anyhow::Result<()>

ScanContext provides config (enabled/paths), Environment provides PATH
and home dir. Conversation struct from model::types already defined,
connectors populate fields and return vec for batch insert.

Idempotency via source_path:
Each connector sets Conversation.source_path to original log/DB file
path. Storage layer uses (agent_id, external_id) UNIQUE constraint to
detect duplicate scans—if source_path unchanged, skip re-parsing. This
enables "cargo run -- index --incremental" to only process new/modified
files, critical for large corpora where full rescan would take minutes.

Error handling strategy:
Individual connector parse failures should not crash indexer—wrap each
conversation parse in anyhow::Context, log error with source file path,
continue to next file. Accumulate parse error count and display in UI
status bar or final summary. For example, if Gemini checkpoint JSON has
unexpected schema, log "Failed to parse checkpoint-abc123.json: missing
'messages' field" and skip that file, continue scanning other checkpoints.

This commit contains only module declarations (mod.rs with pub mod
statements) and empty placeholder files (codex.rs, claude_code.rs,
gemini.rs, cline.rs, amp.rs, opencode.rs with minimal comments) to
establish the connectors/ namespace per implementation checklist
(PLAN § 14.4: Codex connector first for MVP, then Cline, then Gemini,
then remaining three for v1). Actual detection heuristics and parsing
logic will be implemented in connector-specific commits after storage
layer provides upsert API and indexer orchestrates scan flow.

Related: PLAN_TO_MAKE_CODING_AGENT_SESSION_SEARCH.md § 2 (agent history
research), § 3.1 (connectors component), § 7 (detailed connector specs),
§  10 (auto-detection), § 14.4-14.6 (implementation order), README.md
(connectors/ module description).
